title: Estimate derivatives
description: Given a table of values of a function, find the best estimate for the derivative of a function at a given point.
topic_path: Differentiation: definition and basic derivative rules
Estimating derivatives of a function at a point

assessment_items.question_content:
This table gives select values of the differentiable function $f$.

$x$ | $0$ | $3$ | $5$ | $6$ | $9$
:- | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$f(x)$ | $72$ | $95$ | $112$ | $77$ | $54$

**What is the best estimate for $f'(4)$ we can make based on this table?**

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints:
For any function $f$, there are two common interpretations for $f'(c)$, each with a corresponding interpretation for the estimate of $f'(c)$.

| $f'(c)$ is... | An estimate of $f'(c)$ is...
:- | :- | :-
**Numeric interpretation** | ...the *instantaneous* rate of change of $f$ at $x=c$ | ...the *average* rate of change of $f$ over an interval whose endpoints are close to $x=c$
**Graphical interpretation** | ...the slope of the line *tangent* to the graph of $f$ at $x=c$ ![Function f is graphed. The positive x-axis includes value c. The graph is a curve. The curve starts in quadrant 2, moves downward to a point in quadrant 1, moves upward through a point at x = c, and ends in quadrant 1. A tangent line starts in quadrant 4, moves upward, touches the curve at the point at x = c, and ends in quadrant 1.](web+graphie:${â˜£ LOCALPATH}/images/7d63663f52d73f96d377bd6ae86dcd35076b0e35) | ...the slope of a line *secant* to the graph of $f$ whose endpoints are close to $x=c$ ![Function f is graphed. The positive x-axis includes value c. The graph is a curve. The curve starts in quadrant 2, moves downward through a point left of x = c, moves upward through a point right of x = c, and ends in quadrant 1. A secant line starts in quadrant 3, moves upward, intersects the curve at the 2 points, and ends in quadrant 1. The secant line appears to have a similar slope to the tangent in the previous image, although they are not exacty the same.](web+graphie:${â˜£ LOCALPATH}/images/d281adcd16d06c20b66d62318b5843dddbf430c9)

Either interpretation (numeric or graphical) leads to the same estimate of $f'(c)$.
In conclusion, the best estimate for $f'(4)$ is $8.5$.

The curve starts in quadrant 2, moves downward through a point left of x = c, moves upward through a point right of x = c, and ends in quadrant 1. A secant line starts in quadrant 3, moves upward, intersects the curve at the 2 points, and ends in quadrant 1. The secant line appears to have a similar slope to the tangent in the previous image, although they are not exacty the same.](web+graphie:${â˜£ LOCALPATH}/images/d281adcd16d06c20b66d62318b5843dddbf430c9)

Either interpretation (numeric or graphical) leads to the same estimate of $f'(c)$.
In conclusion, the best estimate for $f'(-11)$ is $-3.67$.
